data.content:
### Basic Progress component usage

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/progress.mdx

Renders a basic Progress component with a value of 33, displaying a progress bar at 33% completion. The value prop represents the progress percentage from 0 to 100.

```typescript
<Progress value={33} />
```

--------------------------------

### Import Progress component in React

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/progress.mdx

Imports the Progress component from the local components directory. This allows you to use the Progress component in your React application after installation.

```typescript
import { Progress } from "@/components/ui/progress"
```

--------------------------------

### Install Radix UI Progress dependency

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/progress.mdx

Installs the @radix-ui/react-progress package required for manual installation of the Progress component. This is needed before copying the component source code into your project.

```bash
npm install @radix-ui/react-progress
```

--------------------------------

### Install Progress component via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/progress.mdx

Installs the Progress component from shadcn/ui using the CLI tool. This is the quickest installation method that automatically adds the component to your project.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add progress
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate from `tailwindcss-animate` to `tw-animate-css` in CSS

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This diff illustrates the change required in the `globals.css` file when migrating from the deprecated `tailwindcss-animate` plugin to `tw-animate-css`. It shows the removal of the `@plugin` directive for the old library and the addition of an `@import` statement for the new one.

```diff
- @plugin 'tailwindcss-animate';
+ @import "tw-animate-css";
```

--------------------------------

### Update React Peer Dependencies to Support React 19

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/react-19.mdx

This diff shows the required changes in a package's `peerDependencies` section within `package.json` to include support for React 19. It extends the accepted `react` and `react-dom` versions to include `^19.0`, resolving potential peer dependency conflicts.

```diff
"peerDependencies": {
-  "react": "^16.8 || ^17.0 || ^18.0",
+  "react": "^16.8 || ^17.0 || ^18.0 || ^19.0",
-  "react-dom": "^16.8 || ^17.0 || ^18.0"
+  "react-dom": "^16.8 || ^17.0 || ^18.0 || ^19.0"
},
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Form State and Pending Status with useActionState (React/Next.js)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/forms/next.mdx

This React component demonstrates using `React.useActionState` to manage form submissions, capture form state, and track the `pending` status. The `pending` status is used to disable form inputs and display loading indicators during server action execution.

```tsx
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import Form from "next/form"

import { Spinner } from "@/components/ui/spinner"

import { bugReportFormAction } from "./actions"

export function BugReportForm() {
  const [formState, formAction, pending] = React.useActionState(
    bugReportFormAction,
    {
      errors: null,
      success: false,
    }
  )

  return (
    <Form action={formAction}>
      <FieldGroup>
        <Field data-disabled={pending}>
          <FieldLabel htmlFor="name">Name</FieldLabel>
          <Input id="name" name="name" disabled={pending} />
        </Field>
        <Field>
          <Button type="submit" disabled={pending}>
            {pending && <Spinner />} Submit
          </Button>
        </Field>
      </FieldGroup>
    </Form>
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Update Components for New Dark Mode Colors using CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This command line snippet shows how to update all components in a project using the `shadcn` CLI. The `--overwrite` flag is crucial as it will replace existing components, making it suitable for adopting new dark mode colors or other component updates. Users are advised to commit changes before running this command.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add --all --overwrite
```

--------------------------------

### Install New shadcn-ui Calendar Blocks

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/calendar.mdx

After successfully upgrading the main Calendar component, this command is used to install any new associated blocks or sub-components. It ensures that the latest UI elements and features for the calendar are available in the project. This step complements the upgrade process by bringing in updated block definitions.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add calendar-02
```

--------------------------------

### Replace Tailwind `w-* h-*` with `size-*` Utility

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This diff demonstrates the migration from using separate `w-*` and `h-*` Tailwind utility classes to the new `size-*` utility. The `size-*` utility, introduced in Tailwind v3.4, is fully supported by `tailwind-merge` and provides a more concise way to define both width and height.

```diff
- w-4 h-4
+ size-4
```

--------------------------------

### Update Project Dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This command updates several project dependencies to their latest versions using pnpm. It targets packages from `@radix-ui/*`, `cmdk`, `lucide-react`, `recharts`, `tailwind-merge`, and `clsx` to ensure the project uses the most recent features and bug fixes.

```bash
pnpm up "@radix-ui/*" cmdk lucide-react recharts tailwind-merge clsx --latest
```

--------------------------------

### Check for shadcn/ui component updates

Source: https://context7.com/shadcn-ui/ui/llms.txt

Detect available updates for installed components using the diff command. Can check all components or specific ones, displaying which files have updates available.

```bash
# Check all components for updates
npx shadcn diff

# Check specific component for updates
npx shadcn diff button

# Example output when updates are found:
# The following components have updates available:
# - button
#   - components/ui/button.tsx
# Run diff <component> to see the changes.
```

--------------------------------

### Update Chart Configuration Color Definitions

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This diff shows how to update `chartConfig` by removing the `hsl()` wrapper from color definitions. This is possible because theme colors now include `hsl()` directly, streamlining color usage in chart configurations.

```diff
const chartConfig = {
  desktop: {
    label: "Desktop",
-    color: "hsl(var(--chart-1))",
+    color: "var(--chart-1)",
  },
  mobile: {
    label: "Mobile",
-   color: "hsl(var(--chart-2))",
+   color: "var(--chart-2)",
  },
} satisfies ChartConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Understand npm Peer Dependency Resolution Error

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/react-19.mdx

This code block illustrates the typical `ERESOLVE` error message from npm when a project uses React 19, but an installed package's `peerDependencies` do not explicitly list React 19 as a supported version. This indicates a conflict that needs to be resolved manually.

```bash
npm error code ERESOLVE
npm error ERESOLVE unable to resolve dependency tree
npm error
npm error While resolving: my-app@0.1.0
npm error Found: react@19.0.0-rc-69d4b800-20241021
npm error node_modules/react
npm error   react@"19.0.0-rc-69d4b800-20241021" from the root project
```

--------------------------------

### Before: React Component using `forwardRef`

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This code snippet shows a React functional component, `AccordionItem`, before refactoring. It utilizes `React.forwardRef` to pass a ref down to the underlying DOM element, along with `React.ElementRef` and `React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef` for type definitions, and sets a `displayName`.

```tsx
const AccordionItem = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <AccordionPrimitive.Item
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("border-b last:border-b-0", className)}
    {...props}
  />
))
AccordionItem.displayName = "AccordionItem"
```

--------------------------------

### After: React Component without `forwardRef`

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/tailwind-v4.mdx

This code snippet presents the refactored `AccordionItem` component, demonstrating how to remove `React.forwardRef`. It converts the component into a standard functional component, uses `React.ComponentProps` for props typing, and adds a `data-slot` attribute for styling purposes with Tailwind.

```tsx
function AccordionItem({
  className,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Item>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Item
      data-slot="accordion-item"
      className={cn("border-b last:border-b-0", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Remix theme action route

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/dark-mode/remix.mdx

Create an action route at `/routes/action.set-theme.ts` that handles requests to change the theme. This route utilizes `createThemeAction` from `remix-themes` to interact with the theme session resolver, storing the user's preferred theme in the session storage upon interaction.

```tsx
import { createThemeAction } from "remix-themes"

import { themeSessionResolver } from "./sessions.server"

export const action = createThemeAction(themeSessionResolver)
```
